question:
Why is "virtue signaling" criticized even when the cause being supported is genuinely worthwhile?
A) Supporting good causes is always ethically suspect.
B) When the primary motivation appears to be self-presentation rather than the cause itself, it risks reducing serious issues to social currency.
C) Virtue signaling is illegal in most democracies.
D) Publicly supporting a cause automatically delegitimizes it.
